defects: Spelling/fidelity: Header: Rendered as 'NEW SPECIALTY HOSPITAL – RIYADH' (all caps) vs expected 'New Specialty Hospital — Riyadh' (case not considered an error, em dash vs en dash acceptable, not an error).
Spelling/fidelity: Benefit: Rendered as 'Accommodation' (missing 'Free' from 'Free accommodation').
Spelling/fidelity: Interview: Rendered as 'Prometric + online' and the prompt 'at Apply now' appears missing or replaced with a QR code area—not directly stating 'at Apply now'.
Spelling/fidelity: Email: Rendered as 'jobs@alyousufent.com', but part of the email is covered by a white overlay, making the domain partially obscured.

Missing fact: Country: Saudi Arabia (only appears in the header 'Saudi Arabia Healthcare', which is separated from the factual listing—this may be borderline, but it is not presented alongside the core fact block).
Missing fact: Phone: 9324995767 (not visible anywhere in the advert).
Legibility: Part of the email address is covered by a white overlay, which reduces confidence in full accuracy.
Legibility: The white overlay obscures some text in the lower right—potentially additional factual information.

Visual QA (catastrophic): QR code/CTA white block covers and clips underlying essential agency/trust information (AL-YOUSUF ENTERPRISES LLP and part of the contact), making regulatory/agency identity unreadable.
Visual QA (catastrophic): Contact CTA ('Apply now') is partially cut off and the email is split, difficult to quickly find and read.
Visual QA score 76/100 is below the 85 publishable bar
Visual QA: CTA (email, QR) lacks sufficient visual emphasis and feels lost at the lower right; the 'Apply now' is cut and hard to notice.
Visual QA: Trust/verification (agency/reg/license) is poorly integrated, awkwardly layered under a white block.
Visual QA: Significant lower right dead canvas (white rectangle for the QR/CTA cuts off part of content and creates imbalance).
Visual QA: The large salary header does not mention a destination or project (no employer or country in dominant type).
Visual QA: The candidate hook is not optimally strong; 'Earning SAR 8,400' is not as powerful as 'Healthcare Careers in Saudi Arabia' or project/role focused headline.

Ensure footer and CTA are cleanly readable—reduce conflicts with background and overlays.
